name: Text to CAD
description: Use the open source text-to-cad harness to generate, regenerate, inspect, and review CAD models with coding agents.

thumbnail.alt: Text to CAD harness demo showing generated CAD geometry in the local viewer

access.label: Local CAD harness
access.detail: Requires a local checkout of earthtojake/text-to-cad, Python 3.11 with the CAD requirements installed, and npm for the CAD Explorer viewer. BuildingHub only records setup metadata; model-provider credentials and generated CAD artifacts stay in the agent workspace.

tools.name: git
tools.detail: Clone or inspect the text-to-cad harness repository.

tools.name: python3.11
tools.detail: Create the CAD virtual environment and run bundled CAD/URDF generation scripts.

tools.name: npm
tools.detail: Install and run the CAD Explorer viewer from the viewer/ package.

tools.name: CAD_DIR
tools.detail: Optional CAD Explorer scan directory for production builds; defaults to models.

tools.name: VIEWER_PORT
tools.detail: Optional CAD Explorer port; defaults to 4178.

endpoints.name: CAD skill guide
endpoints.detail: Agent-facing CAD generation, validation, and prompt-reference rules.

endpoints.name: URDF skill guide
endpoints.detail: Agent-facing URDF generation and validation rules.

endpoints.name: CAD Explorer viewer
endpoints.detail: Local Vite viewer for generated STEP, STL, DXF, GLB/topology, and URDF assets.

capabilities.name: git
capabilities.detail: Clone or inspect the text-to-cad harness repository.

capabilities.name: python3.11
capabilities.detail: Create the CAD virtual environment and run bundled CAD/URDF generation scripts.

capabilities.name: npm
capabilities.detail: Install and run the CAD Explorer viewer from the viewer/ package.

capabilities.name: CAD_DIR
capabilities.detail: Optional CAD Explorer scan directory for production builds; defaults to models.

capabilities.name: VIEWER_PORT
capabilities.detail: Optional CAD Explorer port; defaults to 4178.

variables.label: Harness checkout
variables.value: git clone of earthtojake/text-to-cad at a reviewed commit or fork

variables.label: Viewer runtime
variables.value: npm dependencies installed under viewer/ and a local CAD Explorer port

variables.label: CAD workspace
variables.value: models/ by default, or CAD_DIR for an alternate scan root

variables.label: Model credentials
variables.value: only if the chosen coding agent/provider needs them; keep credentials outside BuildingHub

steps.title: Clone or choose a harness
steps.detail: Use the upstream text-to-cad repo or a reviewed project fork as the CAD workspace.

steps.title: Install CAD dependencies
steps.detail: Create the Python 3.11 virtualenv and install requirements-cad.txt before running CAD tools.

steps.title: Install the viewer
steps.detail: Install npm dependencies under viewer/ and run the CAD Explorer locally when visual inspection is needed.

steps.title: Install the building
steps.detail: Add Text to CAD to Agent Town once the harness, Python runtime, and viewer path are ready.

steps.title: Route agents through the guide
steps.detail: Agents should read the generated Text to CAD building guide plus the repo AGENTS.md and bundled CAD/URDF skills before editing models.

agentGuide.summary: Use Text to CAD when an agent needs to turn design intent into source-controlled CAD, regenerate STEP/STL/DXF/GLB/topology artifacts, inspect `@cad[...]` references, or review geometry in the local CAD Explorer.
useCases: Set up a local text-to-cad harness for a new CAD modeling task.
Create or edit Python generator sources under models/ and regenerate explicit CAD outputs.
Package imported STEP/STP files with viewer-ready GLB and topology artifacts.
Generate or validate URDF robot descriptions when the task is about links, joints, limits, or mesh references.
Open CAD Explorer to inspect generated STEP, STL, DXF, GLB/topology, or URDF assets.
Resolve `@cad[...]` refs from viewer feedback before making precise geometry edits.
setup: Start from the harness root and read AGENTS.md, skills/cad/SKILL.md, and skills/urdf/SKILL.md before modifying models.
Prefer ./.venv/bin/python after creating the Python 3.11 CAD runtime from requirements-cad.txt.
Treat generator Python sources and imported STEP/STP files as source of truth; do not hand-edit generated STEP, STL, GLB, topology, DXF, URDF, or viewer artifacts.
Regenerate explicit targets only. Do not run directory-wide CAD generation.
Keep temporary review images outside models/, usually under /tmp/.
Use CAD Explorer at http://localhost:4178 for visual inspection and copied `@cad[...]` refs, then resolve refs with skills/cad/scripts/cadref before editing.
Record the harness commit, exact generation command, source path, generated artifact paths, and viewer/snapshot evidence when CAD output matters to a result.

commands.label: Check harness commit
commands.detail: Run from the harness root to record the exact source revision.

commands.label: Install CAD runtime
commands.detail: Creates the local Python environment for build123d, OCP, CAD scripts, and URDF helpers.

commands.label: Run CAD Explorer
commands.detail: Starts the local viewer; open http://localhost:4178 and use ?dir=<repo-relative-dir> for a non-default CAD directory.

commands.label: Regenerate STEP part
commands.detail: Regenerates one explicit generated part source or imported STEP/STP target.

commands.label: Regenerate STEP assembly
commands.detail: Regenerates one explicit assembly source or imported assembly STEP/STP target.

commands.label: Regenerate DXF
commands.detail: Regenerates a Python source that exposes gen_dxf().

commands.label: Regenerate URDF
commands.detail: Regenerates an explicit URDF source and prints a compact robot/link/joint check.

commands.label: Inspect CAD ref
commands.detail: Resolves a viewer-copied prompt reference from source STEP data.

commands.label: Render review snapshot
commands.detail: Writes a quick PNG for visual review without storing temporary images under models/.

env.name: CAD_DIR
env.detail: Optional CAD Explorer build scan directory; defaults to models.

env.name: VIEWER_PORT
env.detail: Optional CAD Explorer port; defaults to 4178.

env.name: GUI_PORT
env.detail: Alternate viewer port variable accepted by the Vite config when VIEWER_PORT is unset.

env.name: PORT
env.detail: Fallback viewer port variable accepted by the Vite config when VIEWER_PORT and GUI_PORT are unset.
